bead.title: build(feat-031): redacted audit lineage rendering
bead.description:
PROBLEM: Audit lineage metadata fields rendered at ui/src/routes/tenants/[tenant]/databases/[database]/audit/+page.svelte:397-434 (decision, policy_version, schema_version, approver.actor, approver.user_id, reason, origin.surface, origin.tool_name) display as plain <code>/<strong> text without going through the redaction renderer. For contractor users whose backend policy strips commercial fields, restricted values still leak into the DOM via these lineage panel fields.

CURRENT STATE:
- Shared redaction renderer is at ui/src/lib/redaction.ts (redactValue, REDACTED_PLACEHOLDER, isRedactedPlaceholder).
- Existing redaction pattern: ui/src/routes/.../audit/+page.svelte:57-60 defines safeJson(payload, collection) which calls redactValue(payload, redactedFieldsFor(collection)). Used at lines 437-442 for data_before/data_after. Unchanged by this bead.
- Lineage metadata block at audit/+page.svelte:397-434 does not call redactValue() on any metadata field. The "approval summary" surface in this product is precisely these scattered fields (decision, approver.*, reason, origin.*) — there is no separate component for it.
- Sibling pattern is axon-c3895a14 (shared redacted field renderer with no DOM leakage).
- Operator vs contractor view distinction is enforced by the backend policy/grant system; the server returns a per-request redaction list. The UI is responsible only for applying that list before rendering, not for role-based branching.

PROPOSED FIX: Wrap each lineage metadata field at audit/+page.svelte:397-434 with redactValue() (using the redaction list returned by redactedFieldsFor(collection), mirroring the safeJson pattern). Add Playwright coverage in ui/tests/e2e/intent-audit-lineage.spec.ts that exercises a contractor-policy fixture and asserts both the rendered "[redacted]" placeholder and DOM-leakage absence per the axon-c3895a14 sibling pattern.

NON-SCOPE:
- Standalone diff renderer (no separate component exists; before/after JSON side-by-side stays as-is).
- Backend policy or redaction-list shape changes (server is the source of truth).
- Operator-view changes; only contractor-view redaction is asserted here. Operator path is covered by the existing 8 tests, which must continue to pass.
- Refactoring the scattered metadata fields into a new dedicated component (out of scope; redaction is per-field).

Rollback: revert the metadata-field redaction calls atomically; the existing data_before/data_after redaction is independent and need not change.
bead.acceptance:
AC1. Lineage metadata fields rendered at ui/src/routes/tenants/[tenant]/databases/[database]/audit/+page.svelte:397-434 (decision, policy_version, schema_version, approver.actor, approver.user_id, reason, origin.surface, origin.tool_name) pass through redactValue() before display, using the redaction list returned by the intent-detail GraphQL query.
AC2. Existing redaction of data_before/data_after at audit/+page.svelte:437-442 is unchanged.
AC3. ui/tests/e2e/intent-audit-lineage.spec.ts adds a test named "contractor lineage redacts metadata fields" asserting that, for a fixture user with contractor policy, lineage metadata fields render "[redacted]" for restricted values and plain text for permitted ones.
AC4. Same spec adds DOM-leakage assertions per the axon-c3895a14 sibling pattern: redacted values are absent from DOM snapshots, window.localStorage / window.sessionStorage, copied text (clipboard simulation), and console logs (page.evaluate inspections).
AC5. The existing 8 tests in ui/tests/e2e/intent-audit-lineage.spec.ts continue to pass for the operator role.
AC6. cd ui && pnpm test:e2e intent-audit-lineage passes.
AC7. cd ui && pnpm lint && pnpm check passes.
